title: heterotheca villosa var. depressa
intro: Heterotheca villosa var. depressa, also known as Hill's goldeneye or hairy heterotheca, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is commonly found in dry prairies and open woods in North America, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Heterotheca villosa var. depressa can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in herbal supplements to support overall health and well-being.
